subtopics.title: Measurement of Physical Quantities
common_misconceptions: Mass and weight are the same
subtopics.teaching_note: Length: metre rule, tape measure, vernier calliper. Mass: beam balance, electronic balance. Time: stopwatch, clock. Volume: measuring cylinder (liquids), formula (regular solids). SI units: length (metre, m), mass (kilogram, kg), time (second, s), temperature (Kelvin, K; Celsius, °C). Conversions: 1 km = 1000 m, 1 m = 100 cm, 1 kg = 1000 g, 1 hour = 3600 s, 1 L = 1000 cm³.

subtopics.title: Types and Effects of Forces
common_misconceptions: Force is needed to keep things moving
Gravity only acts downward on Earth (it pulls towards center)
subtopics.teaching_note: Force: push or pull. Unit: Newton (N). Types: Contact force (friction, tension) and Non-contact (gravity, magnetic, electrostatic). Friction: opposes motion. Useful (walking, braking) and harmful (wear and tear). Gravity: pulls objects towards Earth. Weight = mass × gravity (W = mg). Speed = distance/time. Balanced forces: object at rest or constant speed. Unbalanced: causes acceleration. Nigerian context: road safety (friction between tyres and road), gravity (objects fall down).
